name: Continue @codebase
description: Continue's built-in embeddings indexing of the open repository

developer-org.value: Continue Dev, Inc. (acquired by Cursor, June 2026)
developer-org.comment: Founded June 2023 by Ty Dunn and Nate Sesti (YC S23). Acquired by Cursor in June 2026; the open-source repo is now read-only.

implementation-language.comment: Core is TypeScript/JavaScript (~92% of the repo); the JetBrains plugin adds a Kotlin shell. The indexing pipeline itself is TypeScript. Checked Jul 2026.

first-release.comment: Approximate. Continue was started June 2023 (YC Summer 2023) and the first public VS Code extension shipped mid-2023; the @codebase embeddings feature was added later in 2023. v1.0 in Feb 2025, final v2.0.0 in Jun 2026.

status.comment: Cursor acquired Continue mid-June 2026; the team shipped a final v2.0.0 and set the repo read-only. Cloud data is being deleted after 2026-07-15. The @codebase feature itself was also marked deprecated in the docs prior to shutdown.

license.comment: Apache-2.0; remains under that license after the acquisition.

open-source.comment: Apache-2.0 OSI license (repo is now archived/read-only but still openly licensed).

github-stars.comment: ~34.8k stars as read from the repo on 2026-07-09. Repo is read-only, so the count is effectively frozen.

form-factor.comment: @codebase is the indexing/retrieval engine bundled inside the Continue IDE assistant (VS Code + JetBrains extension); it is not shipped as a separate product.

standalone-usable.comment: The index and @codebase retrieval are welded into Continue; you cannot bolt Continue's index onto another agent or toolchain.

install-mechanism.comment: Installed as the Continue VS Code extension or JetBrains plugin; @codebase indexing runs inside that extension.

retrieval-technique.comment: Combines embeddings-based vector retrieval with keyword (SQLite FTS5) search in one retrieval path, hence hybrid. Also extracts tree-sitter symbols into a code-snippets index.

query-interface.comment: You type @codebase plus a natural-language question in chat; in agent mode the codebase retrieval is invoked as a tool call.

returns.comment: Returns the most relevant code snippets/chunks (nFinal, default 5) to inject as context; also has an extracted-symbols table backing it.

structural-awareness.comment: Uses tree-sitter AST parsing for symbol extraction and for language-aware code chunking (falls back to basic text chunking for unsupported languages).

chunking-strategy.comment: chunkDocument uses a tree-sitter code chunker for supported languages (respecting the embedding model's maxEmbeddingChunkSize) and a basic size-bounded chunker as fallback.

reranking.comment: useReranking defaults to true: first-pass nRetrieve (25) candidates are reranked down to nFinal (5) via an LLM/reranker (cohere, voyage, llm, or free-trial).

index-location.comment: Index is stored locally under ~/.continue/index (LanceDB vectors + SQLite).

incremental-updates.comment: Compares filesystem state against a SQLite tag_catalog table (per-file timestamp + hash) so only changed files are re-indexed.

real-time-watch.comment: A background indexer keeps the index in sync as workspace files change, rather than requiring a manual re-index.

vector-store.comment: Embeddings stored in a local LanceDB index; SQLite (FTS5 + tag_catalog + code-snippets) backs the keyword/metadata side.

embedding-model.value: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (local, via transformers.js) by default; configurable (e.g. voyage-code-3, nomic-embed-text via Ollama)
embedding-model.comment: Default embeddings are computed locally with all-MiniLM-L6-v2. Continue recommends voyage-code-3 for best accuracy; local embeddings were unavailable in the JetBrains IDE historically.

mcp-support.comment: @codebase is an internal context provider, not exposed as an MCP server. Continue can consume external MCP servers, but its own index is not offered over MCP.

agent-compatibility.comment: The @codebase index is usable only by the Continue assistant itself; no other agent can query it.

ide-integrations.comment: Ships as the Continue VS Code extension and JetBrains plugin. Local @codebase embedding/indexing was historically VS Code-only (unavailable in JetBrains).

language-agnostic.comment: Embeddings + keyword search index arbitrary text files (respecting .gitignore/.continueignore). Tree-sitter symbol extraction and AST chunking, however, only cover parser-supported languages.

supported-languages.value: Any text-based files for embedding/keyword retrieval; tree-sitter AST symbol extraction and code chunking limited to parser-supported languages (~20+)

code-leaves-machine.comment: Default configuration computes embeddings locally (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) and stores the index locally, so nothing leaves the machine. If you configure a hosted embedding provider (Voyage/OpenAI) or a hosted reranker (cohere/voyage), chunk text is then sent to that provider.

local-only-option.comment: Fully offline path exists: local embeddings (transformers.js or nomic-embed-text via Ollama) + local LanceDB index. Reranking can be disabled or done by a local LLM.

self-hostable.comment: OSS extension that runs entirely on the developer's own machine; no server component required for @codebase indexing.

telemetry.comment: Historically collected anonymous usage telemetry on by default (can be disabled in config). The final v2.0.0 release removed anonymous telemetry, so current builds effectively collect none.

max-repo-scale.comment: Positioned for high-level questions and folder/pattern context rather than exhaustive whole-repo review; local indexing of very large monorepos is slow. Handles typical to moderately large repos well; not marketed as monorepo-scale.

pricing-model.comment: The extension and @codebase indexing are free/OSS with local embeddings. If you opt into cloud embedding/rerank/LLM providers you pay those API costs (BYOK).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Solo plan (the IDE extension, including @codebase indexing) was free forever; you only pay for any cloud model/embedding APIs you choose to use.

maturity.comment: Reached v1.0 (2025) and v2.0.0 (2026) with ~34k stars and 2.4M+ installs — stable and widely deployed. Not a 5 because the @codebase feature was marked deprecated and the whole project was discontinued after the Cursor acquisition.

active-maintenance.comment: Final v2.0.0 shipped 2026-06-19, after which the repo went read-only (no new issues/PRs) following the Cursor acquisition. No active maintenance as of 2026-07-09.

community-size.comment: ~34.8k GitHub stars, 2.4M+ VS Code installs, and 21k+ Discord members at peak, though the community is now dispersing after the shutdown.
